name: Kumari Puja
description: Worship ceremony of the Royal Kumari during Indra Jatra. The Kumari is carried in a golden palanquin through the old city, blessing crowds and receiving offerings.
route_description: Procession from Kumari Ghar through Indra Chowk, Ason, and back

name: Samyak
description: Grand Buddhist festival where hundreds of Buddha statues from private homes and monasteries are displayed in public squares for collective worship. Occurs once every 5 or 12 years.
route_description: Statues brought from monasteries and homes to central squares

name: Matya
description: Buddhist pilgrimage circuit around the old city of Patan. Devotees visit every Buddhist shrine and monastery in a single day. Includes lighting butter lamps at each site.
route_description: Complete circuit of all Buddhist sites in Patan — covers 150+ shrines in one day

name: Navami Puja at Taleju
description: Secret tantric worship of Taleju Bhawani performed during Dashain. The only day the inner sanctum of Taleju Temple is open to the public. Animal sacrifices offered.

name: Shraddha Ceremony
description: Ancestral rite performed at Pashupatinath and other riverside locations. Offerings of food, water, and pindas (rice balls) to deceased family members.
route_description: Performed at specific ghats along the Bagmati River

name: Homa at Swayambhu
description: Fire ritual performed by Vajracharya priests at the base of Swayambhunath Stupa. Offerings of ghee, grain, and herbs are made to purify the environment and invoke blessings.
route_description: Performed at the main fire altar on the eastern side of the stupa

name: Ihi (Bel Bibaha)
description: Pre-puberty initiation ceremony for Newar girls. The girl is 'married' to the bel fruit (wood apple), symbolizing Vishnu. Ensures she is never truly widowed.
route_description: Procession from home to ceremony hall with the girl dressed as a bride
